ocr.text: The first table presents correlation coefficient values across variables related to diabetes, specifically focusing on Nondiabetic,
Diabetes without retinopathy, and Diabetic retinopathy. For age, the correlation coefficients are −0.181 for Nondiabetic, −0.015
for Diabetes without retinopathy, and 0.047 for Diabetic retinopathy. Glucose shows a correlation of 0.030 for Nondiabetic,
−0.021 for Diabetes without retinopathy, and 0.001 for Diabetic retinopathy. The duration variable is not applicable for
Nondiabetic with a correlation of −0.049 for Diabetes without retinopathy and 0.016 for Diabetic retinopathy. Lastly, HbA1C
displays a correlation of 0.092 for Nondiabetic, −0.177 for Diabetes without retinopathy, and 0.051 for Diabetic retinopathy.
The second table delineates the incidence and characteristics of two bacterial strains, AE-VTEC O157:H7 and Agg-VTEC
O104:H4. The incidence of sporadic cases for AE-VTEC O157:H7 is described as the third bacterial cause of diarrhoea in
developed countries, while Agg-VTEC O104:H4 is extremely rarely reported. Outbreaks of AE-VTEC O157:H7 are frequent,
ranging from a few to over 6000 cases, contrasted by one outbreak of Agg-VTEC O104:H4 with almost 4000 cases.
Variables  Correlation coefficient values
Nondiabetic  Diabetes without retinopathy  Diabetic retinopathy
Duration  —  −0.049  0.016
AE-VTEC O157:H7  Agg-VTEC O104:H4
Incidence of sporadic cases  Third bacterial cause of diarrhoea in developed countries  Extremely rarely reported
Outbreaks  Frequent (from a few to > 6000 cases)  One outbreak (almost 4000 cases)
Age distribution  Young children (elderly)  Middle aged adults
Gender distribution  Equal  Predominance of female
Incubation  1 to 9 days (average 3-5 days)  8 days (interquartile range 7 to 9 days)
Progression to HUS  7-10%  > 20%
Mortality  0.5%  1.4%
Age distribution for AE-VTEC O157:H7 includes young children and the elderly, while Agg-VTEC O104:H4 affects middle-
aged adults. Gender distribution is equal for AE-VTEC O157:H7 but shows a predominance of females for Agg-VTEC
O104:H4. Incubation periods range from 1 to 9 days (average 3-5 days) for AE-VTEC O157:H7, whereas Agg-VTEC O104:H4
has a duration of 8 days (interquartile range 7 to 9 days). The progression to HUS is 7-10% for AE-VTEC O157:H7 and
exceeds 20% for Agg-VTEC O104:H4, with mortality rates at 0.5% and 1.4%, respectively.
